logical_findings: prepared and current_verified are intentionally different states; v0.4.10 can be prepared while v0.4.9 remains current_verified until publish and DOI closeout finish
release-prep mutates local metadata only and must not tag, publish, push, or write DOI closeout metadata
release-publish is guarded and may need retry/polling around GitHub/Zenodo eventual consistency
DOI closeout is atomic: expected closeout paths must be changed/committed together
recommended_next_fixes: Add retry/polling around release-publish post-create checks to avoid false FAIL immediately after GitHub release creation
Keep release-status read-only and let GUI consume it before enabling any release button
Integrate release notes generator after state model so notes can bind to planned/prepared/published transitions

release_commands.help: Validate post-release GitHub and Zenodo state without guessing DOI metadata.
release_commands.name: post-release-check

release_commands.help: Validate release state for a target version.
release_commands.name: release-check

release_commands.help: Block manual release metadata anchor edits without release-prep evidence.
release_commands.name: release-metadata-authority-gate

release_commands.help: Print a release preparation checklist for the current project.
release_commands.name: release-plan

release_commands.help: Validate before-metadata release readiness for a target version.
release_commands.name: release-preflight

release_commands.help:
Prepare release metadata through the supported agentic-kit route.

This edits only local release metadata files. It does not tag, publish,
push, create GitHub releases, or write Zenodo DOI metadata.
release_commands.name: release-prep

release_commands.help: Plan release publishing without live tag/release side effects.
release_commands.name: release-publish

state_matrix.expected_docs: previous current verified release remains authoritative
state_matrix.meaning: Target version is selected; metadata may still reflect previous release.

state_matrix.expected_docs: package version may be target while current verified release remains previous version
state_matrix.meaning: Release metadata anchors contain target version, but tag/release/DOI are not authoritative yet.

state_matrix.expected_docs: metadata can still say DOI pending until verification
forbidden_commands: DOI metadata edits without verified DOI
state_matrix.meaning: Tag and GitHub release exist; Zenodo/version DOI may still be propagating.

state_matrix.expected_docs: closeout write should update all expected DOI closeout files atomically
state_matrix.meaning: post-release-check confirms concept DOI and version DOI.

state_matrix.expected_docs: current verified release should move to target version
forbidden_commands: dropping any expected closeout file from commit
state_matrix.meaning: All DOI closeout files contain target version and version DOI.

state_matrix.expected_docs: target version is current package and current verified release
forbidden_commands: GUI/release follow-up before final handoff if post-merge-check fails
state_matrix.meaning: Release is published, DOI-closed-out and handoff/main are clean.
